Tylophora disticha is an herbaceous vine that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, bronchitis, and asthma, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tylophora disticha include tylophorine, tylophorinone, and tylophorine-9-N-oxide, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tylophora disticha include decoctions, infusions, and tablets, which are used to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Tylanthus distichus is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant belongs to the order Malpighiales. It is a shrub or small tree species that is commonly found in moist and humid environments.
